problem: The `hlx tickets artifacts` command prints only 'No artifacts found.' and 'No step artifacts found.' when the API response is empty. Users need a run ID to fall back to `hlx tickets artifact --run <runId> --step <stepId> --repo <repoKey>`, but the CLI never surfaces that run ID in the empty-result path.

files.why: Primary change target. Contains `cmdTicketsArtifacts()` which owns the empty-result branch (lines 34-36 for items, lines 44-46 for stepArtifactSummary). Currently does not resolve or print run IDs.

anchors.label: getFlag(args, '--run') extracts optional --run flag
API call to /tickets/${ticketId}/artifacts with optional runId query param
items.length > 0 check — success path for artifact items
'No artifacts found.' — empty items output
stepArtifactSummary.length > 0 check — success path for step summaries
Existing follow-up suggestion (only shown when stepArtifactSummary is non-empty)
'No step artifacts found.' — empty stepArtifactSummary output

files.why: Reference pattern for run ID resolution. Lines 29-40 show the existing approach: fetch ticket detail via GET /api/tickets/${ticketId}, then use currentRun?.id ?? runs[0]?.id. Also defines TicketDetail and TicketResponse types (lines 5-10).

anchors.label: TicketDetail type: { currentRun?: { id: string }; runs: Array<{ id: string }> }
TicketResponse type: { ticket: TicketDetail }
let runId = getFlag(args, '--run')
if (!runId) — fallback fetch ticket detail to resolve run ID
ticket.currentRun?.id ?? ticket.runs[0]?.id — run resolution logic
if (!runId) — no runs found for ticket, error exit

files.why: Second reference for run ID resolution. Lines 33-39 duplicate the same TicketDetail type and currentRun?.id ?? runs[0]?.id resolution pattern.

anchors.label: TicketDetail type with currentRun and runs
runId = ticket.currentRun?.id ?? ticket.runs[0]?.id
No runs found error handling

files.why: Command router. Line 119 dispatches to cmdTicketsArtifacts(config, resolved.id, rest). Note: does NOT pass rawRef (unlike cmdTicketsContinue on line 108). The ticketId received by cmdTicketsArtifacts is the resolved internal ID.

anchors.label: case 'artifacts': — command dispatch block
const rawRef = extractTicketRef(rest)
const resolved = await resolveTicket(config, rawRef)
await cmdTicketsArtifacts(config, resolved.id, rest) — only internal ID passed
Usage: hlx tickets artifacts <ticket-ref> [--run <runId>]

files.why: hxFetch is the HTTP client used for all API calls. Throws on failure (with retries). Understanding throw behavior is relevant to the error-handling requirement.

anchors.label: hxFetch signature with options: { method, body, queryParams, basePath }
throw new Error(message) — non-retryable or last-attempt failure

files.why: Shows the full TicketDetail type with runs array structure including status, startedAt, finishedAt fields (lines 14-20). Wider type than artifact.ts uses.

files.why: Existing test file demonstrating test patterns (Node.js built-in test runner with describe/it/assert). Reference for any new tests.
Second existing test file showing testing conventions.

facts: artifacts.ts cmdTicketsArtifacts() is the sole function handling `hlx tickets artifacts`. It receives (config, ticketId, args) where ticketId is already resolved to an internal ID.
The empty-result branch is when both data.items.length === 0 AND data.stepArtifactSummary.length === 0. Currently outputs 'No artifacts found.' and 'No step artifacts found.' with no run ID.
The --run flag is optional. When provided, it is passed as a query parameter to the API. When absent, the API infers the run on the server side.
artifact.ts (singular) already has a working run-ID resolution pattern: fetch GET /api/tickets/${ticketId}, read ticket.currentRun?.id ?? ticket.runs[0]?.id.
bundle.ts also duplicates the same run-ID resolution pattern with the same TicketDetail type.
The TicketDetail type for run resolution is: { currentRun?: { id: string }; runs: Array<{ id: string }> } — defined locally in both artifact.ts and bundle.ts.
index.ts passes resolved.id (internal ID) as ticketId to cmdTicketsArtifacts, but does NOT pass rawRef. cmdTicketsContinue receives rawRef as a separate parameter.

The existing follow-up suggestion on line 43 of artifacts.ts uses placeholder tokens: 'hlx tickets artifact <ticket-id> --step <stepId> --repo <repoKey>' — shown only when stepArtifactSummary is non-empty.
Quality gates: typecheck (tsc --noEmit) passes clean; test (tsc && node --test) passes 30/30. No lint config.
Project uses TypeScript 6.x with strict mode, ES2022 target, Node16 module resolution. Node.js built-in test runner (describe/it).
No ORM, no migrations, no database. This is a pure CLI tool.
hxFetch throws on network/server errors after retrying (3 attempts with exponential backoff). The ticket requires catching such failures gracefully in the run-ID resolution path.
CI publishes to npm on tag push. Tests run as part of publish pipeline.
unknowns: Whether the /tickets/${ticketId}/artifacts API response includes any runId field that could avoid the extra ticket-detail request. The ArtifactsResponse type only declares items[] and stepArtifactSummary[] — no runId field is typed.

Whether the follow-up command suggestion's <ticket-ref> placeholder should be replaced with the actual internal ticketId or remain a placeholder for the user. The existing line-43 pattern uses literal placeholder tokens.
Whether tests should be added for cmdTicketsArtifacts — currently no test files exist under src/tickets/. The existing tests cover only lib/ utilities.
